info.title: REST API for Common Features in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications - Setup Task CSV Exports/Export Process
info.description:
Last Updated May 2026

You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications.

Whether you're experienced with or new to REST, use this guide to find what you need, including:

  • A quick start how-to that walks you through a simple request example.
  • Use cases that provide real-world solutions with code examples.
  • Detailed descriptions for each REST resource.

Some REST resources have more than one version. In the Tasks section, when you expand a resource that has more than one version, you'll notice that the main folder contains subfolders, such as 11.13.18.05 and v1 (latest), corresponding to the available resource versions. Resources that don't have a newer version continue to appear without any change in their folder structure. You can find additional details on how to work with the different resource versions in the Quick Start and other topics in the Get Started section.
info.x-summary: Last Updated May 2026 You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications.

parameters.name: TaskCode
parameters.description: The short code of the setup task. This attribute is required.

parameters.name: dependency
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies dependencies which are attributes that are set before and rolled back after generating the response. Generally they are used to preview the effects of an attribute change. The attributes specified in this parameter are always set in the resource instance in question. When a child resource collection is requested and the parameter is set, the attributes will be set in the parent resource instance before generating the resource collection payload. The value of this query parameter is a set of dependency attributes. Example: dependency=ProductId=2

Format: <attr1>=<val1>,<attr2>=<value2>

parameters.name: onlyData
parameters.description: The resource item payload will be filtered in order to contain only data (no links section, for example).

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: When this parameter is provided, the specified children are included in the resource payload (instead of just a link). The value of this query parameter is "all" or "". More than one child can be specified using comma as a separator. Example: ?expand=Employees,Localizations. Nested children can also be provided following the format "Child.NestedChild" (Example: ?expand=Employees.Managers). If a nested child is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?expand=Employees.Managers" is the same as "?expand=Employees,Employees.Managers" (which will expand Employees and Managers).

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
This parameter filters the resource attributes. Only the specified attributes are returned, which means that if no attributes are specified, no attributes are returned (useful to get only the links). If an indirect child resource is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?fields=Employees.Managers:Empname" is the same as "?fields=;Employees:;Employees.Managers:Empname" (which will only return the "Empname" field for Managers). the value of this query parameter is a list of resource attributes. The attribute can be a direct (Example: Employees) or indirect (Example: Employees.Managers) child. It cannot be combined with expand query parameter. If both are provided, only fields will be considered.

Format: ?fields=Attribute1,Attribute2

Format for fields in child resource: ?fields=Accessor1:Attribute1,Attribute2

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: This parameter restricts the number of resources returned inside the resource collection. If the limit exceeds the resource count then the framework will only return the available resources.

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description: This parameter orders a resource collection based on the specified attributes.  The parameter value is a comma-separated string of attribute names, each optionally followed by a colon and "asc" or "desc".  Specify "asc" for ascending and "desc" for descending. The default value is "asc".  For example, ?orderBy=field1:asc,field2:desc

parameters.name: totalResults
parameters.description: The resource collection representation will include the "estimated row count" when "?totalResults=true", otherwise the count is not included.  The default value is "false".

parameters.name: q
parameters.description:
This query parameter defines the where clause. The resource collection will be queried using the provided expressions. The value of this query parameter is one or more expressions. Example: ?q=Deptno>=10 and <= 30;Loc!=NY

Format: ?q=expression1;expression2

parameters.name: finder
parameters.description:
Used as a predefined finder to search the collection.

Format: ?finder=<finderName>;<variableName>=<variableValue>,<variableName2>=<variableValue2>

The following are the available finder names and corresponding finder variables:

  • PrimaryKey
    Finder Variables:
    • ProcessId

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: This parameter defines the starting position of the resource collection. If offset exceeds the resource count then no resources are returned. Default value is 0.

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

parameters.name: TaskCode
parameters.description: The short code of the setup task. This attribute is required.

requestBody.description: The following table describes the body parameters in the request for this task.

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

parameters.name: TaskCode
parameters.description: The short code of the setup task. This attribute is required.

parameters.name: ProcessId
parameters.description: The identifier of the export process. This attribute is required.

parameters.name: dependency
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies dependencies which are attributes that are set before and rolled back after generating the response. Generally they are used to preview the effects of an attribute change. The attributes specified in this parameter are always set in the resource instance in question. When a child resource collection is requested and the parameter is set, the attributes will be set in the parent resource instance before generating the resource collection payload. The value of this query parameter is a set of dependency attributes. Example: dependency=ProductId=2

Format: <attr1>=<val1>,<attr2>=<value2>

parameters.name: onlyData
parameters.description: The resource item payload will be filtered in order to contain only data (no links section, for example).

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: When this parameter is provided, the specified children are included in the resource payload (instead of just a link). The value of this query parameter is "all" or "". More than one child can be specified using comma as a separator. Example: ?expand=Employees,Localizations. Nested children can also be provided following the format "Child.NestedChild" (Example: ?expand=Employees.Managers). If a nested child is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?expand=Employees.Managers" is the same as "?expand=Employees,Employees.Managers" (which will expand Employees and Managers).

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
This parameter filters the resource attributes. Only the specified attributes are returned, which means that if no attributes are specified, no attributes are returned (useful to get only the links). If an indirect child resource is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?fields=Employees.Managers:Empname" is the same as "?fields=;Employees:;Employees.Managers:Empname" (which will only return the "Empname" field for Managers). the value of this query parameter is a list of resource attributes. The attribute can be a direct (Example: Employees) or indirect (Example: Employees.Managers) child. It cannot be combined with expand query parameter. If both are provided, only fields will be considered.

Format: ?fields=Attribute1,Attribute2

Format for fields in child resource: ?fields=Accessor1:Attribute1,Attribute2

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

totalResults.description: The estimated row count when "?totalResults=true", otherwise the count is not included.

offset.description: The offset value used in the current page.

count.description: The number of resource instances returned in the current range.

hasMore.description: Indicates whether more resources are available on the server than the subset returned in the response. If the value is true, then there are more resources to retrieve from the server. The default value is false.

limit.description: The actual paging size used by the server.

links.title: Links
links.description: The link relations associated with the resource instance.

kind.title: kind
kind.description: The kind of the related resource.

rel.title: relation
rel.description: The name of the relation to the resource instance.  Example: self.

name.title: name
name.description: The name of the link to the related resource.

href.title: hyperlink reference
href.description: The URI to the related resource.

changeIndicator.description: Change indicator or the ETag value of the resource instance.

links.title: Links
links.description: The link relations associated with the resource instance.

ProcessId.title: Process ID
ProcessId.description: The unique identifier of the setup task CSV export process.

TaskCode.title: Task Code
TaskCode.description: The unique code of the setup task for which the CSV file is generated.

ProcessCompletedFlag.title: Process Complete
ProcessCompletedFlag.description: Indicates whether the setup task CSV export process is complete. This attribute doesn't have a default value.

ProcessId.title: Process ID
ProcessId.description: The unique identifier of the setup task CSV export process.

ProcessName.title: Process Name
ProcessName.description: Name of the setup task CSV export process.

StatusCode.title: Status Code
StatusCode.description: The status of the setup task CSV export process. The valid values are NOT_STARTED, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, COMPLETED_WARNINGS, and COMPLETED_ERRORS.

FileName.title: File Name
FileName.description: The name of the .zip file containing the setup task CSV files.

FileContent.title: File Content
FileContent.description: The .zip file containing the setup task CSV files.

ProcessLog.title: Process Log
ProcessLog.description: The log containing the processing details of each business object in the setup task CSV export process.

CSVTemplateReport.title: CSV Template Report
CSVTemplateReport.description: The .zip file containing the template report files for the setup task CSV export.

ProcessResultsReport.title: Process Results Report
ProcessResultsReport.description: The report that contains the results for each business object of the task included in the CSV export process.

ProcessResultsSummaryReport.title: Process Results Summary Report
ProcessResultsSummaryReport.description: The summary report for the setup task CSV export process.
